Ingredients:
100g Dried Kappaphycus alvarezii
 1000ml Fresh fruit juice (mango/pineapple)
 160g Coconut sugar
 300g Fresh fruits (diced)
 60ml Lime juice
 600ml Filtered water
How to Make:
1. Wash dried seaweed thoroughly in fresh water to remove salt and debris.
2. Soak seaweed in water for 30 minutes until softened.
3. Boil seaweed in 300ml water at 80-90°C for 45-60 minutes to extract carrageenan.
4. Strain the carrageenan solution through fine mesh cloth.
5. Heat fruit juice to 70°C and dissolve coconut sugar.
6. Combine carrageenan extract with sweetened fruit juice.
7. Add lime juice and stir thoroughly for uniform distribution.
8. Pour into molds while still warm (above 50°C).
9. Add diced fresh fruits to each mold.
10. Refrigerate at 4-8°C for 4-6 hours until fully set.
11. Unmold carefully and serve chilled.

Ingredients:
100g Fresh Kappaphycus alvarezii
 30g Rice flour
 15ml Coconut oil
 3g Sea salt
 2g Black pepper
 1g Turmeric powder
 5g curry leaves (dried)
How to Make:
1. Wash fresh seaweed thoroughly to remove sand and debris.
2. Blanch in boiling water for 30 seconds to remove excess mucilage.
3. Pat dry completely using clean cloth or paper towels.
4. Cut seaweed into uniform chip-sized pieces (5x5cm).
5. Mix rice flour with spices to create seasoning blend.
6. Lightly brush seaweed pieces with coconut oil.
7. Dust evenly with seasoning mixture.
8. Arrange in single layer on dehydrator trays or baking sheets.
9. Dehydrate at 55-60°C for 6-8 hours until crispy.
10. Alternatively, bake at 150°C for 15-20 minutes with careful monitoring.
11. Cool completely before packaging.

Ingredients:
100g Sun-dried Kappaphycus alvarezii
 15g Moringa leaf powder
 10g Spirulina powder
How to Make:
1. Select high-quality dried seaweed free from discoloration.
2. Wash thoroughly and sun-dry for additional 2-3 days to reduce moisture to <10%.
3. Break into smaller pieces for grinding.
4. Mill using commercial grinder to achieve fine powder consistency.
5. Sieve through 100-mesh screen to ensure uniform particle size.
6. Blend with moringa and spirulina powders.
7. Mix thoroughly for 10 minutes for homogeneous distribution.
8. Test moisture content (must be <8% for stability).
9. Package in moisture-proof containers with desiccant packets.

Ingredients:
40g Dried Kappaphycus alvarezii
 400ml Coconut milk (thick)
 100g Jaggery (palm sugar)
 3 leaves Pandan leaves
 4 pods Cardamom pods
 30g Cashew nuts (roasted)
 200ml water
How to Make:
1. Soak dried seaweed in water for 30 minutes.
2. Boil seaweed in 200ml water with pandan leaves for 40 minutes.
3. Strain carrageenan extract through muslin cloth.
4. Heat coconut milk with crushed cardamom over medium heat.
5. Dissolve jaggery in warm coconut milk, stirring continuously.
6. Combine carrageenan extract with sweetened coconut milk.
7. Stir well and bring to a gentle simmer for 5 minutes.
8. Remove from heat and strain to remove cardamom pieces.
9. Pour into individual serving bowls or molds.
10. Allow to cool to room temperature.
11. Refrigerate for 4-6 hours until set.
12. Garnish with roasted cashews before serving.

Ingredients:
500g fresh Kappaphycus alvarezii (guso), cleaned
 1/4 cup white vinegar or cane vinegar
 2 medium tomatoes, diced
 1 medium red onion, finely sliced
 2-inch piece ginger, julienned
 2-3 red chili peppers (siling labuyo), sliced
 Salt and pepper to taste
 Optional: 2 tbsp fermented shrimp (ginamos/bagoong alamang)
 Optional: 1/2 cup coconut milk
How to Make:
1. Wash the seaweed thoroughly with clean water to remove any debris and other seaweed species.
2. Remove any hard or discolored parts.
3. Cut into bite-sized pieces (about 2-3 inches).
4. Bring a pot of water to a rolling boil.
5. Add the guso and blanch for 15-20 seconds only (do not over-boil or it will melt into gel).
6. Immediately drain and rinse with cold water to stop cooking.
7. Squeeze out excess water gently
8. In a large bowl, combine the blanched guso.
9. Add vinegar, tomatoes, onion, ginger, and chili peppers.
10. Season with salt and pepper.
11. Optional: Add fermented shrimp and/or coconut milk for richer flavor.
12. Mix well and let marinate for 10-15 minutes.
13. Serve chilled as a side dish or appetizer.
14. Best consumed fresh; can be refrigerated for up to 3 days.
Minerals:
Iodine: High content
Calcium: 80-100mg
Potassium: 150-200mg
Magnesium: 40-50mg
Phosphorus: 30-40mg

Ingredients:
300g fresh Kappaphycus alvarezii
 3 tbsp calamansi juice or lemon juice
 1 medium tomato, diced
 1 small onion, thinly sliced
 2 tbsp fish sauce (patis) or soy sauce
 1 tsp sugar (optional)
 Fresh cilantro or spring onions for garnish
How to Make:
1. Wash seaweed thoroughly in several changes of water.
2. Cut into desired lengths (2-3 inches).
3. Soak in cold water for 10 minutes if it's very salty.
4. Boil water and blanch seaweed for 10-15 seconds.
5. Drain and plunge into ice water.
6. Drain well and squeeze gently.
7. Combine calamansi juice, fish sauce, and sugar.
8. Mix with seaweed, tomato, and onion.
9. Toss well and let sit for 5 minutes.
10. Garnish with cilantro or spring onions.
11. Serve immediately at room temperature or chilled.
12. Pairs well with grilled fish or meat.

Ingredients:
200g Kappaphycus alvarezii (guso)
 200g lato (sea grapes) - optional
 1/4 cup vinegar
 3 tbsp fish sauce
 2 shallots, minced
 3 cloves garlic, minced
 1-2 bird's eye chilies, chopped
 1 mango, julienned (green or ripe)
 Fresh coconut meat, sliced (optional)
How to Make:
1. Clean seaweed thoroughly and cut into pieces.
2. Blanch seaweed for 15 seconds, drain and cool.
3. If using lato, simply rinse and drain (do not blanch).
4. Mix vinegar, fish sauce, shallots, garlic, and chilies.
5. Let sit for 5 minutes to blend flavors.
6. Toss seaweeds with dressing.
7. Add julienned mango and coconut meat.
8. Mix gently to avoid breaking the sea grapes.
9. Serve immediately as a refreshing appetizer.
10. Best enjoyed fresh.

Ingredients:
400g fresh Kappaphycus alvarezii
 2 cups coconut milk
 1 cup coconut cream
 3 cloves garlic, minced
 1 medium onion, sliced
 2-inch piece ginger, julienned
 2-3 green chilies
 Fish sauce to taste
 Salt and pepper
How to Make:
1. Clean and cut seaweed into 2-inch pieces.
2. Blanch briefly (15-20 seconds) and drain.
3. In a pan, sauté garlic, onion, and ginger until fragrant.
4. Add coconut milk and bring to a gentle simmer.
5. Add the blanched guso to the coconut milk.
6. Add green chilies.
7. Simmer gently for 3-5 minutes (do not overcook).
8. Add coconut cream and fish sauce.
9. Season with salt and pepper.
10. Simmer for 1-2 minutes more.
11. Remove from heat.
12. Serve hot with steamed rice.
13. Best consumed immediately.

Ingredients:
200g fresh Kappaphycus Alvarezii, blanched and cut into strips
 8-10 rice paper wrappers
 1 cup rice vermicelli noodles, cooked
 1 medium carrot, julienned
 1 cucumber, julienned
 Fresh mint leaves
 Fresh cilantro
 Lettuce leaves
 1/4 cup roasted peanuts, crushed
 3 tbsp fish sauce
 2 tbsp lime juice
 1 tbsp sugar
 1 clove garlic, minced
 1 red chili, sliced
How to Make:
1. Prepare all ingredients and have them ready in separate bowls.
2. Dip one rice paper wrapper in warm water for 5 seconds until soft.
3. Lay flat on a clean surface.
4. Place lettuce leaf in center, add noodles, seaweed, vegetables, and herbs.
5. Fold sides in and roll tightly from bottom.
6. Mix all sauce ingredients together.
7. Serve fresh rolls with dipping sauce and crushed peanuts.

Ingredients:
300g fresh Kappaphycus Alvarezii
 6 cups chicken or vegetable broth
 200g tofu, sliced
 2 medium tomatoes, quartered
 1 onion, sliced
 3 cloves garlic, minced
 2-inch piece ginger, sliced
 Fish sauce to taste
 Salt and pepper
 Green onions for garnish
How to Make:
1. In a pot, sauté garlic, onion, and ginger.
2. Add tofu and brown slightly.
3. Add tomatoes and cook until soft.
4. Pour in broth and bring to boil.
5. Add seaweed and simmer for 3-5 minutes (don't overcook).
6. Season with fish sauce, salt, and pepper.
7. Garnish with green onions.
8. Serve hot with rice.

Ingredients:
400g Kappaphycus Alvarezii, blanched
 1/2 cup white vinegar
 1/4 cup sugar
 1 medium carrot, julienned
 1 red bell pepper, julienned
 1 onion, thinly sliced
 2-inch piece ginger, julienned
 1 tsp salt
 1/2 tsp peppercorns
How to Make:
1. Blanch seaweed for 15 seconds, drain well.
2. In a saucepan, combine vinegar, sugar, and salt.
3. Bring to boil until sugar dissolves.
4. Let cool completely.
5. Mix guso with vegetables.
6. Pour cooled vinegar mixture over.
7. Add peppercorns.
8. Store in sterilized jar in refrigerator.
9. Let marinate for at least 2 hours before serving.

Ingredients:
300g fresh Kappaphycus Alvarezii, blanched
 2 tbsp vegetable oil
 3 cloves garlic, minced
 1 onion, sliced
 1 cup broccoli florets
 1 carrot, sliced
 1 red bell pepper, sliced
 2 tbsp oyster sauce
 1 tbsp soy sauce
 1 tsp sesame oil
 Salt and pepper
How to Make:
1. Heat oil in wok over high heat.
2. Sauté garlic and onion until fragrant.
3. Add vegetables and stir-fry for 3 minutes.
4. Add blanched seaweed.
5. Season with oyster sauce, soy sauce.
6. Stir-fry for 2 minutes.
7. Drizzle with sesame oil.
8. Serve immediately with rice.

Ingredients:
2 tbsp guso gel (boiled and blended guso)
 2 frozen bananas
 1 cup frozen berries
 1/2 cup coconut milk or almond milk
 1 tbsp honey
Toppings: Fresh fruits, Granola, Coconut flakes, Chia seeds, Nuts
How to Make:
1. To make guso gel: boil 50g guso in 2 cups water for 10 minutes, blend until smooth.
2. In blender, combine guso gel, frozen bananas, berries, milk, and honey.
3. Blend until smooth and creamy.
4. Pour into bowl.
5. Arrange toppings attractively.
6. Serve immediately.

Ingredients:
400g fresh Kappaphycus Alvarezii, blanched
 3 tbsp chili oil
 2 tbsp black vinegar
 2 tbsp soy sauce
 1 tbsp sesame oil
 4 cloves garlic, minced
 2 tsp sugar
 1 tsp Sichuan peppercorns (optional)
 Green onions, chopped
 Sesame seeds for garnish
How to Make:
1. Blanch seaweed and drain well.
2. Mix chili oil, vinegar, soy sauce, sesame oil, garlic, and sugar.
3. Pour over guso and toss well.
4. Let marinate for 15 minutes.
5. Garnish with green onions and sesame seeds.
6. Serve as appetizer or side dish.

Ingredients:
300g Kappaphycus Alvarezii, blanched and chopped
 4 cups cooked rice (day-old works best)
 3 eggs, beaten
 1 cup mixed vegetables (peas, carrots, corn)
 3 cloves garlic, minced
 2 tbsp soy sauce
 1 tbsp fish sauce
 2 tbsp vegetable oil
 Green onions for garnish
 Sesame seeds
How to Make:
1. Heat oil in large wok or pan.
2. Scramble eggs, remove and set aside.
3. Add more oil if needed, sauté garlic.
4. Add vegetables, stir-fry for 2 minutes.
5. Add rice, breaking up clumps.
6. Add seaweed and mix well.
7. Season with soy sauce and fish sauce.
8. Add scrambled eggs back.
9. Garnish with green onions and sesame seeds.

Ingredients:
300g fresh Kappaphycus Alvarezii, cut into noodle-like strips
 200g noodles, cooked and chilled
 1/4 cup soy sauce
 2 tbsp mirin
 1 tbsp rice vinegar
 1 tsp sesame oil
 Nori sheets, shredded
 Wasabi
 Green onions, sliced
 Sesame seeds
How to Make:
1. Blanch seaweed strips for 10 seconds, cool in ice water.
2. Cook noodles, rinse in cold water.
3. Mix soy sauce, mirin, vinegar, and sesame oil.
4. Combine seaweed and noodles.
5. Pour sauce over and toss.
6. Top with nori, wasabi, green onions, and sesame seeds.
7. Serve chilled.

Ingredients:
200g Kappaphycus Alvarezii, blanched
 300g rice noodles or sotanghon
 200g chicken, sliced
 2 cups cabbage, shredded
 1 carrot, julienned
 1/2 cup green beans
 4 cloves garlic, minced
 1 onion, sliced
 3 tbsp soy sauce
 2 tbsp oyster sauce
 Fish sauce to taste
 Calamansi or lemon wedges
How to Make:
1. Soak noodles in warm water until soft.
2. Heat oil, sauté garlic and onion.
3. Add meat, cook until done.
4. Add vegetables, stir-fry.
5. Add noodles and seaweed.
6. Season with soy sauce, oyster sauce, fish sauce.
7. Toss everything together.
8. Serve with calamansi wedges.

Ingredients:
300g fresh Kappaphycus Alvarezii, cut into small bundles
 1 cup tempura flour or all-purpose flour
 3/4 cup ice-cold water
 1 egg yolk
 Oil for deep frying
Dipping Sauce: 1/4 cup soy sauce, 2 tbsp mirin, 1 tbsp rice vinegar, 1 tsp grated daikon
How to Make:
1. Pat seaweed dry completely.
2. Mix flour, ice water, and egg yolk lightly (lumps are okay).
3. Heat oil to 350°F (175°C).
4. Dip seaweed bundles in batter.
5. Fry until golden and crispy, about 2-3 minutes.
6. Drain on paper towels.
7. Mix dipping sauce ingredients.
8. Serve hot with sauce.

Ingredients:
250g Kappaphycus Alvarezii, blanched and chopped
 20 lumpia wrappers
 200g ground chicken
 1 cup cabbage, shredded
 1 carrot, julienned
 3 cloves garlic, minced
 1 onion, minced
 Soy sauce and salt to taste
 Oil for frying
How to Make:
1. Sauté garlic and onion.
2. Add meat, cook until brown.
3. Add vegetables and seaweed.
4. Season with soy sauce and salt.
5. Let filling cool.
6. Wrap in lumpia wrappers.
7. Seal edges with water.
8. Deep fry until golden.
9. Serve with sweet chili sauce or vinegar.

Ingredients:
100g dried Kappaphycus Alvarezii or 400g fresh Kappaphycus Alvarezii
 6 cups water
 1 cup sugar (adjust to taste)
 1/2 cup condensed milk
 1 cup fruit cocktail or fresh fruits
 Food coloring (optional)
How to Make:
1. If using dried seaweed, soak for 30 minutes.
2. Boil seaweed in water for 20-30 minutes until dissolved.
3. Strain through cheesecloth to remove seaweed.
4. Add sugar while still hot, stir until dissolved.
5. Pour into molds or pan.
6. Refrigerate for 3-4 hours until set.
7. Cut into cubes.
8. Serve with condensed milk and fruits.

Ingredients:
300g Kappaphycus Alvarezii, blanched
 Large lettuce leaves or cabbage leaves
 1 cup cooked rice
 1 avocado, sliced
 1 cucumber, julienned
 1 carrot, julienned
 Hummus or peanut sauce
How to Make:
1. Lay out lettuce or cabbage leaves.
2. Spread thin layer of hummus or peanut sauce.
3. Add rice, seaweed, and vegetables.
4. Roll tightly.
5. Cut in half diagonally.
6. Serve with additional sauce.

Ingredients:
200g fresh Kappaphycus Alvarezii
 2 tbsp olive oil
 1 tsp sea salt
 1/2 tsp garlic powder
 1/2 tsp paprika
 Sesame seeds (optional)
How to Make:
1. Preheat oven to 300°F (150°C).
2. Blanch seaweed very briefly (5-10 seconds).
3. Pat completely dry with paper towels.
4. Spread on baking sheets lined with parchment.
5. Brush with olive oil.
6. Sprinkle with salt, garlic powder, paprika, and sesame seeds.
7. Bake for 15-20 minutes until crispy.
8. Let cool completely (will crisp more as it cools).
9. Store in airtight container.

Ingredients:
200g fresh Kappaphycus Alvarezii, cut into bite-size pieces
 4 cups dashi broth
 3 tbsp miso paste (white or red)
 200g silken tofu, cubed
 2 green onions, sliced
 1 sheet nori, cut into strips
How to Make:
1. Heat dashi broth in a pot.
2. Add seaweed and simmer for 2 minutes.
3. Add tofu cubes.
4. In a small bowl, dissolve miso paste with some hot broth.
5. Add miso mixture to pot (do not boil after adding miso).
6. Garnish with green onions and nori.
7. Serve hot.

Ingredients:
300g fresh Kappaphycus Alvarezii, blanched
 200g fresh fish (tuna, salmon, or white fish), diced
 1/2 cup lime or calamansi juice
 1 red onion, finely diced
 2 tomatoes, diced
 1 cucumber, diced
 1-2 chili peppers, minced
 Fresh cilantro, chopped
 Salt and pepper
How to Make:
1. Dice fish into small cubes.
2. Marinate fish in lime juice for 20-30 minutes.
3. Blanch seaweed briefly, cool and chop.
4. Mix fish, seaweed, onion, tomatoes, cucumber, chili.
5. Add more lime juice if needed.
6. Season with salt and pepper.
7. Add cilantro.
8. Chill for 30 minutes.
9. Serve with crackers or tortilla chips.

Ingredients:
200g Kappaphycus Alvarezii, blanched and chopped
 1 pizza dough
 1/2 cup pizza sauce
 2 cups mozzarella cheese
 1/4 cup parmesan cheese
 Cherry tomatoes, halved
 Fresh basil leaves
 Olive oil
 Garlic, minced
How to Make:
1. Preheat oven to 450°F (230°C).
2. Roll out pizza dough.
3. Spread pizza sauce.
4. Sprinkle mozzarella cheese.
5. Add seaweed, tomatoes, and garlic.
6. Drizzle with olive oil.
7. Bake for 12-15 minutes until crust is golden.
8. Top with parmesan and fresh basil.
9. Serve hot.

Ingredients:
250g Kappaphycus Alvarezii, blanched and chopped
 400g pasta of choice
 3 tbsp olive oil
 4 cloves garlic, sliced
 1/2 tsp red pepper flakes
 1/2 cup white wine (optional)
 1 cup cherry tomatoes, halved
 Fresh parsley, chopped
 Parmesan cheese
 Salt and pepper
How to Make:
1. Cook pasta according to package directions.
2. Heat olive oil in large pan.
3. Sauté garlic until fragrant.
4. Add red pepper flakes.
5. Add white wine, let reduce.
6. Add tomatoes and seaweed.
7. Cook for 3-4 minutes.
8. Add cooked pasta and toss.
9. Season with salt and pepper.
10. Top with parsley and parmesan.

Ingredients:
300g Kappaphycus Alvarezii, blanched
 8 small tortillas
 1 cup cabbage, shredded
 1/2 cup pickled red onions
 1 avocado, sliced
 Fresh cilantro
 Lime wedges
For the guso: 2 tbsp olive oil, 1 tsp cumin
 1 tsp paprika, 1/2 tsp chili powder, Salt and pepper
Sauce: 1/2 cup sour cream or Greek yogurt, 1 tbsp lime juice, 1 tsp sriracha
How to Make:
1. Season seaweed with spices.
2. Sauté in olive oil for 2-3 minutes.
3. Mix sauce ingredients.
4. Warm tortillas.
5. Fill with cabbage, seasoned seaweed, pickled onions, avocado.
6. Drizzle with sauce.
7. Top with cilantro.
8. Serve with lime wedges.

Ingredients:
3 tbsp Kappaphycus Alvarezii gel (see recipe 9)
 1 cup dates, pitted
 1 cup nuts (almonds, cashews, or walnuts)
 2 tbsp cocoa powder
 2 tbsp chia seeds
 1 tbsp honey
 1/4 cup shredded coconut
 Pinch of salt
How to Make:
1. Blend dates and nuts in food processor.
2. Add seaweed gel, cocoa, chia seeds, honey, and salt.
3. Process until mixture sticks together.
4. Roll into balls (about 1 inch diameter).
5. Roll in shredded coconut.
6. Refrigerate for 1 hour.
7. Store in refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.

Ingredients:
100g dried Kappaphycus Alvarezii or 300g fresh
 4 cups coconut milk
 1 cup water
 3/4 cup sugar
 1/4 tsp salt
 Pandan leaves (optional)
 Fresh fruits for topping
How to Make:
1. If using dried seaweed, soak for 30 minutes.
2. Boil seaweed in water for 20 minutes.
3. Strain to extract gel.
4. In a pot, combine coconut milk, seaweed gel, sugar, salt.
5. Add pandan leaves if using.
6. Simmer for 10 minutes, stirring constantly.
7. Remove pandan leaves.
8. Pour into molds or glasses.
9. Refrigerate for 4 hours until set.
10. Top with fresh fruits before serving.

Ingredients:
50g Kappaphycus seaweed (soaked & chopped)
½ cup Grated coconut
1 small Onion (chopped)
1 Green chili
1 tbsp Lime juice 
Salt
How to Make:
1. Soak seaweed 10 min & rinse. 
2. Finely chop seaweed. 
3. Mix coconut, onion, chili. 
4. Add seaweed, salt & lime. 
5. Mix well and serve.

Ingredients:
30g Kappaphycus seaweed
2 Eggs
2 tbsp Onion
1 tsp Oil
Salt
Pepper
How to Make:
1. Heat oil in pan.
2. Sauté onion.
3. Add chopped seaweed, cook 1 min.
4. Beat eggs & pour in.
5. Scramble until cooked.

Ingredients:
40g Seaweed
2 cups Cooked rice
1 tbsp Oil
1 tsp Garlic
Salt
How to Make:
1. Heat oil in pan.
2. Fry garlic.
3. Add chopped seaweed & sauté.
4. Add cooked rice & salt.
5. Mix well and heat through.

Ingredients:
60g Dried Gracilaria edulis
 800ml water
 120g cane sugar
 15ml Rose water
 200g Fresh fruits (mixed)
 5ml Almond extract
How to Make:
1. Wash dried Gracilaria thoroughly, removing any debris.
2. Soak in cold water for 2 hours until fully hydrated.
3. Boil in 800ml water at 85-90°C for 60-75 minutes.
4. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
5. Strain through multiple layers of cheesecloth while hot.
6. Return strained liquid to pot and add sugar.
7. Heat gently until sugar completely dissolves.
8. Remove from heat and add rose water and almond extract.
9. Arrange fresh fruit pieces in serving dishes or molds.
10. Pour warm agar solution over fruits.
11. Allow to cool at room temperature for 30 minutes.
12. Refrigerate for 2-3 hours until firmly set.

Ingredients:
150g Fresh Gracilaria edulis
 20ml Sesame oil
 30ml Rice vinegar
 15ml Soy sauce
 10g Sesame seeds (toasted)
 8g Fresh ginger (grated)
 5g Garlic (minced)
 2g Red chili flakes
 20g Green onions (sliced)
How to Make:
1. Harvest or obtain fresh Gracilaria, selecting tender portions.
2. Wash thoroughly in cold running water (3-4 rinses).
3. Blanch in boiling water for 10-15 seconds only.
4. Immediately transfer to ice bath to stop cooking.
5. Drain completely and gently pat dry.
6. Cut into bite-sized pieces (3-4cm lengths).
7. Prepare dressing by whisking sesame oil, vinegar, and soy sauce.
8. Add grated ginger and minced garlic to dressing.
9. Toss seaweed gently with dressing.
10. Sprinkle with toasted sesame seeds and green onions.
11. Add chili flakes if desired.
12. Refrigerate for 30 minutes before serving to allow flavors to meld.

Ingredients:
30g Dried Gracilaria edulis
 400ml Coconut water
 50g Fresh passion fruit pulp
 40g Raw honey
 25ml Fresh lime juice
 10ml Ginger juice
 200ml Water
How to Make:
1. Rinse dried seaweed thoroughly to remove surface salt.
2. Soak in 200ml water for 1 hour.
3. Simmer gently at 70°C for 30 minutes to extract polysaccharides.
4. Strain through fine mesh, reserving the extract (discard seaweed).
5. Cool extract to room temperature.
6. Blend passion fruit pulp until smooth.
7. Combine seaweed extract with coconut water.
8. Add strained passion fruit, honey, lime juice, and ginger juice.
9. Stir thoroughly until honey is fully dissolved.
10. Strain again if any particles remain.
11. Chill in refrigerator for at least 2 hours.
12. Shake well before serving over ice.

Ingredients:
80g Dried Gracilaria edulis (powdered)
 300g Rice flour
 100g Tapioca starch
 8g Sea salt
 180ml Water
 15ml Vegetable oil
How to Make:
1. Mill dried seaweed into fine powder using commercial grinder.
2. Sieve through 80-mesh screen for uniform particle size.
3. Combine rice flour, tapioca starch, seaweed powder, and salt.
4. Gradually add water while mixing to form dough.
5. Add vegetable oil and knead for 10-15 minutes until smooth.
6. Rest dough covered for 30 minutes at room temperature.
7. Divide dough into portions for rolling.
8. Roll each portion to 2mm thickness using pasta machine or rolling pin.
9. Cut into desired noodle width (3-5mm for standard).
10. Dust lightly with rice flour to prevent sticking.
11. Dry at 50°C for 4-6 hours for shelf-stable product.
12. Alternatively, cook fresh noodles immediately in boiling water for 2-3 minutes.

Ingredients:
200g Fresh Gracilaria edulis
 150ml Rice vinegar
 20g Sea salt
 40g Cane sugar
 30g Fresh ginger (sliced)
 15g Red chili (sliced)
 6 cloves Garlic cloves
 5g Black peppercorns
How to Make:
1. Harvest fresh seaweed, selecting young tender portions.
2. Wash thoroughly in several changes of water.
3. Blanch briefly in boiling water for 20 seconds.
4. Immediately transfer to ice water bath.
5. Drain completely and pat dry.
6. Cut into 2-3cm pieces.
7. Prepare brine by heating vinegar with sugar and salt until dissolved.
8. Add ginger, chili, garlic, and peppercorns to hot brine.
9. Allow brine to cool to room temperature.
10. Pack seaweed into sterilized glass jars.
11. Pour cooled brine over seaweed, ensuring complete coverage.
12. Seal jars and refrigerate for at least 7 days before consuming.

Ingredients:
1 lb fresh Gracilaria edulis
 1 medium onion, thinly sliced
 1/2 cup green onions, chopped
 2 cloves garlic, minced
 3/4 cup soy sauce
 2 tbsp rice vinegar
 1 tsp sesame oil
 1 tsp sugar
 1/4 tsp black pepper
 Hawaiian red chili peppers, sliced (optional)
How to Make:
1. Bring a large pot of water to rolling boil.
2. Place seaweed in colander.
3. Pour boiling water over seaweed for 1-2 minutes only (don't overcook or it will melt).
4. Immediately rinse under cold running water.
5. Soak in ice water for 5 minutes to seal crunchiness.
6. Drain well and squeeze out excess water.
7. Mix all sauce ingredients together.
8. Combine seaweed with onions and garlic.
9. Pour sauce over and toss well.
10. Refrigerate overnight for best flavor.
11. Serve chilled.

Ingredients:
2 lb fresh Gracilaria edulis
 1 cup rice vinegar
 1/2 cup soy sauce
 1/4 cup sugar
 1 tbsp gochujang (Korean chili paste)
 1 onion, sliced
 1/2 cup green onions, chopped
 1/2 cup dried shrimp
 2 Hawaiian red peppers, seeded and chopped
 3 cloves garlic, minced
 1 tbsp sesame seeds
How to Make:
1. Clean seaweed thoroughly.
2. Blanch in boiling water for 1-2 minutes.
3. Rinse in cold water and drain.
4. In large bowl, combine all ingredients.
5. Mix well to coat seaweed evenly.
6. Refrigerate for at least 2 hours.
7. Serve as side dish or condiment.

Ingredients:
1 cup fresh Gracilaria edulis, chopped
 6 oz firm tofu, cubed
 2 small Persian cucumbers, sliced
 1 carrot, julienned
 2 tbsp rice vinegar
 1 tbsp sesame oil
 1.5 tsp honey or agave
 1-inch piece ginger, grated
 Soy sauce to taste
 Sesame seeds for garnish
How to Make:
1. Blanch seaweed briefly and cool.
2. Cut tofu into small cubes.
3. Slice cucumbers and julienne carrot.
4. Mix vinegar, sesame oil, honey, and ginger.
5. Combine all vegetables and tofu.
6. Pour dressing over and toss.
7. Garnish with sesame seeds.
8. Serve chilled.

Ingredients:
4.5 lb fresh or dried Gracilaria edulis
 7.5 quarts water
 21.5 oz sugar (adjust to taste)
 3 limes, juiced
 1 cup coconut cream
 Pinch of salt
How to Make:
1. If using dried seaweed, wash several times.
2. Boil seaweed in water for 15-20 minutes until it dissolves.
3. Strain through cheesecloth to remove solid pieces.
4. Add sugar while hot, stir until dissolved.
5. Add lime juice and salt.
6. Let cool until it begins to set.
7. Stir in coconut cream.
8. Pour into molds or bowls.
9. Refrigerate until completely set (3-4 hours).
10. Serve cold as dessert.

Ingredients:
1 cup fresh Gracilaria edulis, chopped
 1 lb fresh ahi tuna, cubed
 3 cups cooked white or brown rice
 1/4 cup soy sauce
 1 tbsp sesame oil
 1 tsp sriracha
 2 green onions, sliced
 1 avocado, sliced
 1 cucumber, diced
 Sesame seeds
 Furikake seasoning
How to Make:
1. Blanch seaweed briefly, drain and cool.
2. Cut tuna into bite-size cubes.
3. Mix soy sauce, sesame oil, and sriracha.
4. Marinate tuna in sauce for 10 minutes.
5. Place rice in bowls.
6. Top with tuna, seaweed, avocado, cucumber.
7. Sprinkle with green onions, sesame seeds, and furikake.
8. Drizzle with remaining sauce.
9. Serve immediately.

Ingredients:
300g fresh Gracilaria edulis, blanched
 300g chiken, sliced thin
 3 cloves garlic, minced
 1-inch piece ginger, julienned
 2 tbsp soy sauce
 1 tbsp rice wine or sake
 1 tsp sugar
 2 green onions, cut into 2-inch pieces
 2 tbsp vegetable oil
 Salt to taste
How to Make:
1. Blanch seaweed for 1 minute, drain well.
2. Heat oil in wok over high heat.
3. Stir-fry chiken until browned.
4. Add garlic and ginger, cook until fragrant.
5. Add seaweed and stir-fry for 1 minute.
6. Add soy sauce, wine, sugar, and salt.
7. Simmer for 30 minutes until liquid reduces.
8. Add green onions.
9. Can be served hot or cooled and jellied.
10. If jellied, refrigerate and cut into pieces.

Dietary Restrictions: Vegetarian Option / Contains Fish if Dashi used
Ingredients:
200g fresh Gracilaria edulis
 6 cups dashi broth
 3-4 tbsp miso paste (white or red)
 200g silken tofu, cubed
 2 green onions, sliced
 1 sheet nori, cut into strips
 Wakame seaweed (optional)
How to Make:
1. Bring dashi to gentle simmer.
2. Add seaweed and cook for 2 minutes.
3. Add tofu cubes.
4. In small bowl, dissolve miso with some hot broth.
5. Add miso to soup (do not boil after adding).
6. Garnish with green onions and nori.
7. Serve hot immediately.

Ingredients:
300g fresh Gracilaria edulis, formed into small bundles
 1 cup tempura flour or all-purpose flour
 3/4 cup ice-cold water
 1 egg yolk
 Oil for deep frying
Dipping Sauce: 1/4 cup dashi, 2 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tbsp mirin, Grated daikon radish
How to Make:
1. Pat seaweed completely dry.
2. Mix flour, ice water, and egg yolk lightly.
3. Heat oil to 350°F (175°C).
4. Dip seaweed bundles in batter.
5. Fry until golden, 2-3 minutes.
6. Drain on paper towels.
7. Mix dipping sauce ingredients.
8. Serve hot with sauce and daikon.

Ingredients:
1 cup fresh Gracilaria edulis, blanched
 Large lettuce or cabbage leaves
 1 cup cooked rice or quinoa
 1 avocado, sliced
 1 cucumber, julienned
 1 carrot, julienned
 Fresh cilantro and mint
 Peanut sauce or spicy mayo
How to Make:
1. Blanch seaweed briefly and drain.
2. Lay out lettuce leaves.
3. Spread thin layer of sauce.
4. Add rice, seaweed, and vegetables.
5. Top with herbs.
6. Roll tightly.
7. Cut in half diagonally.
8. Serve with additional sauce.

Ingredients:
100g dried Gracilaria edulis or 400g fresh
 6 cups water
 1 cup sugar
 2 tsp white vinegar
 Food coloring (optional)
 1/2 cup condensed milk for serving
 Fresh fruits (optional)
How to Make:
1. Rinse seaweed 5-6 times in fresh water until pink color fades.
2. Boil seaweed with water and vinegar for 20-30 minutes.
3. Strain through cheesecloth.
4. Add sugar while hot, stir until dissolved.
5. Add food coloring if desired.
6. Pour into molds or pan.
7. Refrigerate 3-4 hours until set.
8. Cut into cubes.
9. Serve with condensed milk and fruits.

Ingredients:
250g fresh Gracilaria edulis
 500g firm white fish, cut into pieces
 4 cups fish or vegetable broth
 2 medium tomatoes, quartered
 1 onion, sliced
 3 cloves garlic, minced
 1-inch piece ginger, sliced
 2 tbsp fish sauce
 1 tbsp soy sauce
 Salt and pepper
 Fresh cilantro
How to Make:
1. Sauté garlic, onion, and ginger.
2. Add tomatoes and cook until soft.
3. Pour in broth and bring to boil.
4. Add fish pieces and simmer 5 minutes.
5. Add seaweed and cook 3 minutes.
6. Season with fish sauce, soy sauce, salt, pepper.
7. Garnish with cilantro.
8. Serve hot with rice.

Ingredients:
1 cup fresh Gracilaria edulis, chopped
 2 large cucumbers, thinly sliced
 1/4 cup rice vinegar
 2 tbsp sugar
 1 tsp salt
 1 tsp sesame oil
 Red chili flakes (optional)
 Sesame seeds
How to Make:
1. Blanch seaweed and cool.
2. Slice cucumbers very thin.
3. Mix vinegar, sugar, salt, and sesame oil.
4. Stir until sugar dissolves.
5. Combine seaweed and cucumbers.
6. Pour dressing over.
7. Let marinate 15 minutes.
8. Garnish with chili flakes and sesame seeds.
9. Serve chilled.

Ingredients:
1 cup fresh Gracilaria edulis, blanched
 3 cups cooked rice
 2 eggs
 1/2 cup dashi broth
 2 tbsp soy sauce
 1 tbsp mirin
 1 tsp sugar
 Green onions, sliced
 Nori strips
How to Make:
1. Mix dashi, soy sauce, mirin, and sugar.
2. Heat in pan and add seaweed.
3. Simmer for 2 minutes.
4. Beat eggs lightly.
5. Pour eggs over ogo mixture.
6. Cover and cook until eggs are just set.
7. Place rice in bowls.
8. Top with seaweed-egg mixture.
9. Garnish with green onions and nori.
10. Serve hot.

Ingredients:
250g fresh Gracilaria edulis, blanched
 300g fresh fish (tuna, mahi-mahi, or snapper), diced
 1/2 cup lime juice
 1 red onion, finely diced
 2 tomatoes, diced
 1 jalapeño, minced
 Fresh cilantro, chopped
 Salt and pepper
 Coconut milk (optional)
How to Make:
1. Dice fish into small cubes.
2. Marinate fish in lime juice 20-30 minutes.
3. Blanch seaweed briefly, cool and chop.
4. Mix fish, seaweed, onion, tomatoes, jalapeño.
5. Add cilantro.
6. Season with salt and pepper.
7. Optional: add splash of coconut milk.
8. Chill 30 minutes.
9. Serve with crackers or chips.

Ingredients:
200g fresh Gracilaria edulis, blanched
 12 spring roll wrappers
 200g ground chicken or pork
 1 cup cabbage, shredded
 1 carrot, julienned
 3 cloves garlic, minced
 Soy sauce and fish sauce
 Oil for frying
How to Make:
1. Sauté garlic, add meat and cook.
2. Add vegetables and seaweed.
3. Season with soy and fish sauce.
4. Let filling cool.
5. Place filling on wrapper.
6. Roll tightly, seal with water.
7. Deep fry until golden.
8. Serve with sweet chili sauce.

Ingredients:
2 cups fresh Gracilaria edulis, blanched
 2 cups cabbage, shredded
 1 cup carrots, shredded
 1/2 cup arame or other seaweed (garnish)
 1/4 cup rice vinegar
 2 tbsp sugar
 1 tbsp soy sauce
 1 tsp sesame oil
 Salt and pepper
How to Make:
1. Blanch seaweed and drain well.
2. Shred cabbage and carrots.
3. Mix vinegar, sugar, soy sauce, sesame oil.
4. Combine all vegetables except arame.
5. Pour dressing over and toss.
6. Top with arame as garnish.
7. Serve chilled.

Ingredients:
300g fresh Gracilaria edulis
 3 medium tomatoes, diced
 1 large onion, sliced
 2 tbsp white vinegar
 1 tsp salt
 1-2 green chilies, sliced
 Fresh lime juice
How to Make:
1. Wash seaweed thoroughly.
2. Can be used raw or blanch briefly.
3. Chop into bite-size pieces.
4. Mix with tomatoes and onion.
5. Add vinegar, salt, and chilies.
6. Squeeze lime juice over.
7. Toss well and let sit 10 minutes.
8. Serve as side dish.

Ingredients:
200g fresh Gracilaria edulis
 200g ramen or udon noodles
 6 cups chicken or vegetable broth
 2 eggs, soft-boiled
 1 cup bok choy or spinach
 3 green onions, sliced
 2 cloves garlic, minced
 1 tbsp soy sauce
 1 tsp sesame oil
 Nori sheets
How to Make:
1. Bring broth to boil.
2. Add garlic and soy sauce.
3. Cook noodles according to package.
4. Add seaweed and greens, cook 2 minutes.
5. Divide noodles and broth into bowls.
6. Top with halved soft-boiled eggs.
7. Garnish with green onions and nori.
8. Drizzle with sesame oil.
9. Serve hot.

Ingredients:
300g fresh Gracilaria edulis
 2 tbsp olive oil or coconut oil
 1 tsp sea salt
 1/2 tsp garlic powder
 1/2 tsp onion powder
 Optional: sesame seeds, chili flakes
How to Make:
1. Preheat oven to 300°F (150°C).
2. Blanch seaweed very briefly (5 seconds).
3. Pat completely dry with paper towels.
4. Spread on parchment-lined baking sheets.
5. Brush with oil.
6. Sprinkle with seasonings.
7. Bake 15-20 minutes until crispy.
8. Let cool completely.
9. Store in airtight container.

Ingredients:
2 tbsp fresh Gracilaria edulis (or 1 tbsp ogo gel)
 1 frozen banana
 1 cup frozen mango or pineapple
 1 cup coconut water or almond milk
 1 tbsp honey
 1 tsp chia seeds
 Juice of 1/2 lime
How to Make:
1. If using fresh seaweed, blanch and blend into gel.
2. Add all ingredients to blender.
3. Blend until smooth and creamy.
4. Add more liquid if too thick.
5. Pour into glass.
6. Optional: top with coconut flakes.
7. Serve immediately.

Ingredients:
400g fresh Gracilaria edulis
 1 cup white or rice vinegar
 1/4 cup sugar
 2 tbsp salt
 1-inch piece ginger, julienned
 2 cloves garlic, sliced
 1 tsp peppercorns
 Red chili peppers (optional)
How to Make:
1. Blanch seaweed for 10 seconds.
2. Drain and cool completely.
3. In saucepan, combine vinegar, sugar, and salt.
4. Bring to boil until sugar dissolves.
5. Let cool completely.
6. Place seaweed in sterilized jar.
7. Add ginger, garlic, peppercorns, chilies.
8. Pour cooled vinegar mixture over.
9. Seal jar and refrigerate.
10. Ready to eat after 24 hours.
11. Keeps for 2-3 weeks refrigerated.

Ingredients:
250g fresh Gracilaria edulis, blanched
 400g spaghetti or linguine
 4 tbsp olive oil
 6 cloves garlic, thinly sliced
 1/2 tsp red pepper flakes
 1 cup cherry tomatoes, halved
 1/4 cup white wine (optional)
 Fresh parsley, chopped
 Parmesan cheese
 Salt and black pepper
How to Make:
1. Cook pasta according to package.
2. Heat olive oil in large pan.
3. Sauté garlic until fragrant (don't burn).
4. Add red pepper flakes.
5. Add white wine, let reduce.
6. Add tomatoes and seaweed.
7. Cook 3-4 minutes.
8. Add cooked pasta, toss well.
9. Season with salt and pepper.
10. Top with parsley and parmesan.
11. Serve hot.

Ingredients:
1/2 cup fresh Gracilaria edulis, chopped
 4 eggs
 2 tbsp milk
 2 green onions, sliced
 1/4 cup cheese (optional)
 Salt and pepper
 1 tbsp butter or oil
How to Make:
1. Blanch seaweed briefly and drain.
2. Beat eggs with milk, salt, and pepper.
3. Heat butter in non-stick pan.
4. Pour in half the egg mixture.
5. Add seaweed, green onions, and cheese.
6. Cook until edges set.
7. Fold omelet in half.
8. Cook until golden.
9. Repeat for second omelet.
10. Serve hot.

Ingredients:
2 tbsp Gracilaria edulis gel (boiled and blended)
 1 cup dates, pitted
 1/2 cup almonds
 1/2 cup cashews
 2 tbsp cocoa powder
 1 tbsp chia seeds
 1 tbsp honey
 Shredded coconut for coating
How to Make:
1. Prepare seaweed gel by boiling and blending.
2. Process dates and nuts in food processor.
3. Add seaweed gel, cocoa, chia, and honey.
4. Blend until mixture sticks together.
5. Roll into 1-inch balls.
6. Roll in shredded coconut.
7. Refrigerate 1 hour.
8. Store in fridge up to 2 weeks.

Ingredients:
100g dried Gracilaria edulis or 300g fresh
 3 cups coconut milk
 1 cup water
 3/4 cup sugar
 1/4 tsp salt
 Pandan leaves (optional)
 Fresh tropical fruits for topping
How to Make:
1. Boil seaweed in water until dissolved (20 minutes).
2. Strain through cheesecloth.
3. In pot, combine coconut milk, seaweed gel, sugar, salt.
4. Add pandan leaves if using.
5. Simmer 10 minutes, stirring constantly.
6. Remove pandan leaves.
7. Pour into molds or glasses.
8. Refrigerate 4 hours until set.
9. Top with fresh fruits.
10. Serve chilled.

Ingredients:
40g Gracilaria 
2 ups Water 
1 clove Garlic
¼ tsp Pepper
Salt
How to Make:
1. Boil water. 
2. Add garlic & seaweed.
3. Simmer 5 min.
4. Add salt & pepper.
5. Serve warm.

Ingredients:
20 g Gracilaria
2 cups Water
1 tsp Honey (optional)
How to Make:
1. Rinse seaweed well. 
2. Boil in water 5 min. 
3. Strain liquid. 
4. Add honey if needed. 
5. Drink warm or cool.

Ingredients:
100g Noodles 
40g Gracilaria 
½ cup Vegetables
1 tbsp Soy sauce
How to Make:
1. Boil noodles & drain. 
2. Lightly cook vegetables. 
3. Add seaweed. 
4. Add noodles & soy sauce. 
5. Toss well and serve.
